subject: Re: stripey stripe stripe
body:
Hi All

Thanks to all above. I noticed that the conversion worked, either by IMOD 
or relion_converto_to_tiff, but I found that Stacker does not work when 
converting to tiff. 

Thanks
Mike

On Friday, March 19, 2021 at 12:53:03 AM UTC Pranav Shah wrote:

> I realised that I had replied only to mike.. so copying the reply here for 
> completeness -
> ===
> Hi Mike,
>
> You could run -
> 1. clip brightness -n 1 in.mrc out.mrc or,
> 2. e2proc3d.py in.mrc out.mrc
> ===
>
> Best,
> Pranav
> --
> Pranav Shah
> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>
> Division of Structural Biology,
> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> University of Oxford,
> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> UK
>
> On Thu, Mar 18, 2021 at 11:39 PM Mathew McLaren <cla...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Mike,
>>
>> Not Pranav, but I also had this problem and my workaround was to convert 
>> the files to tiff. I tried using the IMOD alterheader command but never 
>> figured out the correct one to make the mrc files work for me.
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Mat
>>
>> On Thu, 18 Mar 2021 at 14:52, Mike Lockhart <m.p.lo...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Pranav
>>>
>>> I'm having the same problem with this. How does one downgrade the mrc 
>>> header? Thanks in advance.
>>>
>>> Best
>>> Mike
>>>
>>> On Thursday, February 25, 2021 at 12:45:30 PM UTC p.sha...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> To update the group.
>>>> I was able to resolve this by "downgrading" the mrc headers so that 
>>>> they are interpretable by Warp. Hopefully Dimitry will have a fix for this.
>>>>
>>>> On Saturday, January 9, 2021 at 3:01:10 PM UTC Pranav Shah wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi Alister,
>>>>>
>>>>> Thanks for checking in. It doesnt appear that I am motion correcting 
>>>>> with too fine spatial resolution.I crossed-checked all the values and they 
>>>>> are in accordance with the header information in the files.
>>>>>
>>>>> Best,
>>>>> Pranav
>>>>> --
>>>>> Pranav Shah
>>>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>>>
>>>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>>>> University of Oxford,
>>>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>>>> UK
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>> On Sat, Jan 9, 2021 at 1:51 PM Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> 
>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi Pranav,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I’ve noticed when reconstructing tomograms the images wrap around in 
>>>>>> the way you described if reconstructing an area larger than the images - Is 
>>>>>> it possible that you’re motion correcting with too fine spatial resolution 
>>>>>> and ending up with large shifts, causing this wrap around to fill the empty 
>>>>>> space?
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Alister
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On 9 Jan 2021, at 12:41, Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>> ﻿
>>>>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>>>> S
>>>>>> o I took a look a closer look at the images and it appears that 
>>>>>> during motion correction in warp, the image is wrongly spliced and the 
>>>>>> stripe at the end of the image should actually be in the beginning. I ran 
>>>>>> motion correction with 1x1x8 grid and defocus with 2x2x1 grid so I dont 
>>>>>> think that should be the cause of the problem. In parallel I also processed 
>>>>>> a dataset collected on a K2 with similar preproc setttings and I dont see 
>>>>>> this transposition/splicing so it seems to be a falcon4 mrc file thing. I 
>>>>>> hope this information somehow helps in diagnosing the problem.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>> Pranav
>>>>>> On Tuesday, January 5, 2021 at 8:51:49 PM UTC Pranav Shah wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Apologies for the spam, it seems I cannot share data more than 8mb 
>>>>>>> on the forum, a decimated version of the image is now attached
>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>> Pranav
>>>>>>> --
>>>>>>> Pranav Shah
>>>>>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>>>>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>>>>>> University of Oxford,
>>>>>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>>>>>> UK
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> On Tue, Jan 5, 2021 at 8:13 PM Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> 
>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Bugger, the image failed to attach... try this one...
>>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>> Pranav
>>>>>>>> --
>>>>>>>> Pranav Shah
>>>>>>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>>>>>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>>>>>>> University of Oxford,
>>>>>>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>>>>>>> UK
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> On Tue, Jan 5, 2021 at 8:12 PM Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> 
>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> I am processing a Falcon 4 dataset and i am consistently seeing a 
>>>>>>>>> band in the right third of image following motion correction. I dont see 
>>>>>>>>> this artefact when I processed the same dataset using MotionCor2, could you 
>>>>>>>>> help understand what is causing this and if should fret about it? Image 
>>>>>>>>> attached for reference.
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>>> Pranav
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> --
